currentStep.openingLine: Let's change the structure one line at a time so we do not lose which terms still match.
currentStep.firstTutorQuestion: After removing the parentheses, which terms are actually alike and can be combined?
currentStep.smallHint: Only terms with the same variable part can combine, and the outside sign still affects every term inside the parentheses.
currentStep.goodStoppingPoint: Learner simplifies a linear expression and can explain why unlike terms did not combine.
watchFor: Learner removes parentheses without tracking the outer sign or factor.
Learner combines unlike terms or changes the coefficient while collecting terms.
currentStep.exampleTutorMove: Have the learner rewrite the expression with parentheses removed, then circle only the terms that still match structurally.
currentStep.exampleLearnerResponse: After removing the parentheses I get 3x + 2x - 4, so only the x terms combine and the result is 5x - 4.
currentStep.title: Simplify without breaking structure
